title: Nested Data Structures
summary: Represent real-world data with lists of dictionaries and nested containers.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Nested Data Structures
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Nested data structures allow us to represent complex data by combining lists, dictionaries, and other containers. For example, a list of dictionaries can represent a collection of records, where each dictionary holds key-value pairs for a single record.

```python
# Example of a list of dictionaries
students = [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 24, 'major': 'Physics'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'age': 22, 'major': 'Mathematics'},
    {'name': 'Charlie', 'age': 23, 'major': 'Biology'}
]

# Accessing data
for student in students:
    print(f"{student['name']} is studying {student['major']}")
```

Try it yourself: Modify the example to add a new student to the list and print their details.

Line by line: read each statement from top to bottom. Notice which names are created, which values are passed into functions, and what output the code is meant to show.

quizDraft.title: Accessing Nested Data
quizDraft.prompt: How do you access the 'age' of the second student in the list `students`?
quizDraft.hint: Use indexing to access the second dictionary and then the key.

help.concept: Accessing nested data involves using indices and keys.
help.hint_1: First, access the second dictionary using an index.
help.hint_2: Then, use the key 'age' to get the value.

quizDraft.title: Modifying Nested Data
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following will change the major of 'Charlie' to 'Chemistry'?
quizDraft.hint: Find the dictionary for 'Charlie' and update the 'major' key.

help.concept: To modify nested data, locate the correct dictionary and update the key-value pair.

quizDraft.title: Accessing Nested List
quizDraft.prompt: Complete the code to access the 'major' of the last student in the list `students`.
quizDraft.hint: Use negative indexing to access the last element.

help.concept: Negative indexing allows access to elements from the end of a list.
help.hint_1: Use -1 to access the last element of the list.

quizDraft.title: Modifying Nested Dictionary
quizDraft.prompt: Complete the code to change the 'age' of 'Alice' to 25.
quizDraft.hint: Find the dictionary for 'Alice' and update the 'age' key.

help.concept: To modify a nested dictionary, locate the correct dictionary and update the key-value pair.
help.hint_2: Update the 'age' key to the missing term.
